archimate:ApplicationInterface.name: Log Management
archimate:ApplicationInterface.documentation:
Definition: A Log Management Application Service refers to a web-based service that facilitates the collection, storage, analysis, and monitoring of log data generated by web applications, servers, and other web-based systems. It provides a centralized platform accessible via the web, allowing users to aggregate, search, and visualize log data for troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and security analysis purposes. The service typically includes features such as log ingestion, parsing, indexing, and customizable dashboards, providing users with insights into their web-based systems' operational status, identifying errors, anomalies, and potential security threats.
Source: The Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP)
Source reference: https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/10-Logging_Testing/02-Log_Management_Best_Practices
LOST view: TVA-API Enablers [Motivation]
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/LogManagementApplicationInterface
ABB name: egovera:LogManagementApplicationInterface
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/LogManagementApplicationInterface
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/WebServiceApplicationInterface

properties.value: A Log Management Application Service refers to a web-based service that facilitates the collection, storage, analysis, and monitoring of log data generated by web applications, servers, and other web-based systems. It provides a centralized platform accessible via the web, allowing users to aggregate, search, and visualize log data for troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and security analysis purposes. The service typically includes features such as log ingestion, parsing, indexing, and customizable dashboards, providing users with insights into their web-based systems' operational status, identifying errors, anomalies, and potential security threats.
The Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP)
